title: Read a file
instruction:
`fs::read_to_string` reads an entire file into a String.

Run this.
successMessage: fs::read_to_string is the simplest way to read a file. It returns Result<String, io::Error> — we unwrap here but in production you'd handle the error.

title: Write a file
instruction:
`fs::write` creates or overwrites a file.

Run this.
successMessage: fs::write takes anything that can be converted to bytes. It creates the file if it doesn't exist and overwrites if it does.

title: Handle file errors
instruction:
Use match or ? to handle file not found and other errors.

Run this.
successMessage: Pattern matching on Result handles both outcomes. The file might not exist — Rust forces you to decide what to do in that case.

title: Append to a file
instruction:
`OpenOptions` lets you open files with specific modes.

Run this.

codeChecks.message: Use .append(true) to add to the end of the file without truncating.

successMessage: OpenOptions is a builder for file modes. append(true) adds to the end. create(true) creates the file if it doesn't exist.

title: Read line by line
instruction:
`BufReader` reads efficiently line by line without loading the whole file.

Run this.
successMessage: BufReader wraps a File with buffered reading. .lines() returns an iterator of Result<String> — each line is read lazily.

title: Paths
instruction:
`Path` and `PathBuf` handle file paths in a cross-platform way.

Run this.
successMessage: Path is borrowed (like &str). PathBuf is owned (like String). push() extends the path safely regardless of OS separator.

title: fs::read for binary
instruction:
`fs::read` returns `Vec<u8>` — useful for binary files.

Run this.
successMessage: fs::read returns raw bytes. String::from_utf8 converts if valid UTF-8. For truly binary data (images, etc.) you work directly with Vec<u8>.

title: Walk a directory
successMessage: read_dir returns an iterator of Result<DirEntry>. We extract the file name, collect into a Vec, then sort for deterministic output.
